checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=3035651852.44 (2011-12-31) -> AccountsReceivableNet=3543128197.54 (2012-12-31), a 16.7% increase, versus Revenues=15584801500.66 (FY2011) -> Revenues=16941558047.57 (FY2012), an 8.7% increase

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Revenues grew only 8.7% over the same period (Revenues=15584801500.66 FY2011 to Revenues=16941558047.57 FY2012), and DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations rose from 21254100344.30 (2011-12-31) to 29013342182.25 (2012-12-31), consistent with a large debt-funded acquisition

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=157715175.78 (2011-12-31) -> InventoryNet=198562839.14 (2012-12-31), a 25.9% increase versus 8.7% revenue growth, but inventory is <1% of total Assets=43732424878.93 (2012-12-31)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 balance sheet, InventoryNet and Assets

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly swung from -282468634.81 (2010 Q1) to 294178298.30 (2010 Q3), and ProfitLoss swung from -242159972.80 (2012 Q1) to 456216753.21 (2012 Q3)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=28994989843.57 (2011-12-31) -> Liabilities=39023571979.14 (2012-12-31), a 34.6% increase, outpacing 8.7% revenue growth; DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations rose from 21254100344.30 to 29013342182.25 over the same period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2010-03-01, filed just 3 days after 10-K filing_date=2010-02-26

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInInvestingActivities and NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities series only populated through 2011-03-31 quarterly / FY2010 annual, with no 2011 full-year or FY2012 figures provided
No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccounts, CostOfGoodsSold, or gross margin line items are present in the provided series

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition, extended customer credit terms, or channel stuffing causing receivables to outpace billed revenue growth in FY2011 and FY2012, which would overstate revenue/receivables and understate any needed allowance buildup

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=3035651852.44 (2011-12-31), up 19.2% from AccountsReceivableNet=2547679092.80 (2010-12-31), while Revenues grew only 9.7% (Revenues=14202843354.00 FY2010 to Revenues=15584801500.66 FY2011)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2011/2012, balance sheet and income statement

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNet=3543128197.54 (2012-12-31), up 16.7% from 2011-12-31, while Revenues grew only 8.7% (Revenues=15584801500.66 FY2011 to Revenues=16941558047.57 FY2012)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, balance sheet and income statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase-accounting allocation from a large debt-funded acquisition completed in FY2012 may have overvalued acquired goodwill/intangibles relative to underlying cash-generating ability, creating future impairment risk and potentially overstated non-current assets in the interim

rationale_evidence.quote: Goodwill=736373504.18 (2011-12-31) jumped to Goodwill=2520107389.55 (2012-12-31), a 242% increase, and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4812012499.82 (2011-12-31) rose to 7572520126.05 (2012-12-31), a 57% increase, versus only 8.7% revenue growth

DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations rose from 21254100344.30 (2011-12-31) to 29013342182.25 (2012-12-31), consistent with debt-financed acquisition activity coinciding with multiple SC TO-T tender offer amendments filed Aug-Nov 2012
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2012 balance sheet; filing_chronology SC TO-T entries 2012

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large and persistent gap between reported net income and operating cash flow (driven largely by high non-cash depreciation/amortization typical of a capital-intensive business) increases the risk that earnings quality is being managed via discretionary D&A, impairment timing, or other non-cash adjustments rather than reflecting core cash economics

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, income statement and cash flow statement

top_signals: Accounts receivable grew roughly 2x faster than revenue in both FY2011 (19.2% vs 9.7%) and FY2012 (16.7% vs 8.7%)
Goodwill (+242%) and intangibles (+57%) surged in FY2012 far outpacing 8.7% revenue growth, coinciding with a large debt increase and tender-offer (SC TO-T) acquisition activity
Persistent large divergence between net income/profit and operating cash flow across all years (e.g., FY2012 OCF over 11x net income)
Filing chronology shows NT 10-K forms filed on the same date as the corresponding 10-K in both 2009 and 2013, plus a 10-K/A filed only 3 days after the FY2009 10-K
